title: Determinističko = hook/kapija, nikad prompt
lesson_sr: Kad pitanje traži 'determinističko' sprovođenje pravila, odgovor je programski mehanizam (hook, prerequisite gate), NE prompt instrukcija. Prompt ima nenulti procenat greške - model 'povremeno' ne posluša. Ako pitanje izričito kaže da model ponekad greši, to je signal da prompt-bazirane opcije otpadaju.
lesson_en: When a question asks for 'deterministic' enforcement, the answer is a programmatic mechanism (hook, prerequisite gate), NOT a prompt instruction. Prompts have a non-zero failure rate. If the question states the model 'occasionally' misbehaves, that is the tell that prompt-based options are wrong.

title: Prevencija hvata PRE akcije, ne posle
lesson_sr: Da bi SPREČIO štetnu akciju, moraš je presresti PRE izvršenja (PreToolCall). Hvatanje posle (PostToolUse) znači da je šteta već nastala - to je čišćenje/oporavak, ne prevencija. Uvek proveri KADA mehanizam deluje u odnosu na akciju.
lesson_en: To PREVENT a harmful action you must intercept BEFORE execution (PreToolCall). Catching it after (PostToolUse) means the damage is already done - that is cleanup/recovery, not prevention. Always check WHEN the mechanism acts relative to the action.

title: Oslanjaj se na strukturirane signale, ne na parsiranje teksta
lesson_sr: Kontrolu toka (npr. kraj agentic petlje) vodi preko strukturiranih signala, nikad preko parsiranja prirodnog jezika iz odgovora. Parsiranje teksta (fraza, XML tag) i fiksni iteration cap-ovi su anti-paterni; čak i stop_sequence je loš jer i dalje zavisi od NL generisanja. ALI pazi KOJI strukturni signal: stop_reason je za turn/loop-kontrolu (nastavi dok je tool_use, stani na end_turn), dok je za eksplicitan završetak ZADATKA pravi signal namenski completion tool, ne end_turn (vidi [[task-completion-tool-not-end-turn]]).
lesson_en: Drive control flow (e.g. agentic loop termination) via structured signals, never by parsing natural language from the response. Text parsing (a phrase, an XML tag) and fixed iteration caps are anti-patterns; even a stop_sequence is poor because it still depends on NL generation. BUT mind WHICH structured signal: stop_reason is for turn/loop control (continue while tool_use, stop on end_turn), whereas for explicit TASK completion the right signal is a dedicated completion tool, not end_turn (see task-completion-tool-not-end-turn).

title: Cilj je sprovesti pravilo, ne ugasiti funkciju
lesson_sr: Rešenje koje potpuno uklanja sposobnost (npr. samo read-only tool) je obično preterano. Politika traži uslovljen tok rada (dry-run -> odobrenje -> izvršenje), a ne zabranu. Čuvaj legitimnu funkcionalnost iza kapije, ne briši je.
lesson_en: A solution that removes a capability entirely (e.g. read-only only) is usually over-broad. The policy wants a gated workflow (dry-run -> approval -> execute), not a ban. Keep legitimate functionality behind a gate, do not delete it.

title: Uskladi dekompoziciju sa prirodom zadatka
lesson_sr: Strategiju dekompozicije biraj prema PRIRODI zadatka, ne univerzalno. Strukturiran/predvidljiv/ponovljiv zadatak -> fiksni sekvencijalni pipeline (jeftin, deterministički, pouzdan). Otvoren/kompleksan/varijabilan zadatak -> dinamička adaptivna dekompozicija (model sam odlučuje korake). Kad sistem ima zadatke različite prirode, koristi MEŠAVINU - i proveri da je raspored tačan. Zamka: opcija sa istim obrascem za sve zadatke pada; opcija sa pravom idejom ali obrnutim rasporedom je najopasniji distraktor.
lesson_en: Choose the decomposition strategy by the NATURE of the task, not one-size-fits-all. Structured/predictable/repeatable task -> fixed sequential pipeline (cheap, deterministic, reliable). Open-ended/complex/variable task -> dynamic adaptive decomposition (the model decides the steps). When a system has tasks of different natures, use a MIX - and verify the mapping is correct. Trap: the option using one pattern for all tasks fails; the option with the right idea but reversed mapping is the most dangerous distractor.

title: Garanciju daje API parametar, ne prompt
lesson_sr: Kad pitanje traži da se GARANTUJE / 'always' ponašanje modela, rešenje je strukturni parametar u API zahtevu, ne prompt instrukcija. Za iznuđivanje poziva alata to je tool_choice ('any' = mora neki alat, 'tool'+name = mora baš taj). Zamke: temperature kontroliše nasumičnost izlaza, NE tip izlaza (tekst vs tool_use); prompt instrukcija je probabilistička (model 'uglavnom' posluša); i pazi na opcije koje izmišljaju nepostojeći 'native feature'. Isti deterministički koren kao [[deterministic-needs-hook-not-prompt]].
lesson_en: When a question asks to GUARANTEE / 'always' a model behavior, the answer is a structured parameter in the API request, not a prompt instruction. To force a tool call that is tool_choice ('any' = must use some tool, 'tool'+name = must use that one). Traps: temperature controls output randomness, NOT output type (text vs tool_use); a prompt instruction is probabilistic (the model 'usually' complies); and watch for options inventing a nonexistent 'native feature'. Same deterministic root as deterministic-needs-hook-not-prompt.

title: Podatke između agenata prenosi kao strukturu, ne kao prozu
lesson_sr: Kada jedan agent prosleđuje podatke drugom, koristi strukturni format (JSON/CSV), ne 'conversational summary' na prirodnom jeziku. Proza je lossy: gube se tačni brojevi, polja postaju dvosmislena, agent-primalac ne može pouzdano da rekonstruiše vrednosti. Struktura čuva tačne vrednosti i značenje polja preko granica između agenata. Rođak [[structured-signals-over-text]] (tamo za kontrolu toka, ovde za prenos podataka).
lesson_en: When one agent hands data to another, use a structured format (JSON/CSV), not a natural-language 'conversational summary'. Prose is lossy: exact numbers are lost, fields become ambiguous, and the receiving agent cannot reliably reconstruct values. Structure preserves exact values and field semantics across agent boundaries. Sibling of structured-signals-over-text (that one for control flow, this one for data transfer).

title: Reši UZROK, ne simptom ('most robust' = ukloni uzrok)
lesson_sr: Kada pitanje traži 'most robust' / 'best' arhitektonsko rešenje, biraj ono koje uklanja KOREN problema, ne ono koje detektuje ili ublažava posledicu posle. Simptomatska rešenja (validacija/provera POSLE akcije, retry petlje, niženje temperature, dodavanje još mehanizama odozgo) zvuče korisno ali ostavljaju uzrok netaknut. Posebna zamka: opcija koja kaže 'pre nego što se prikaze korisniku' zvuči kao prevencija, ali ako samo detektuje grešku koja je već nastala - to je reaktivno čišćenje, ne prevencija. Srodno sa [[intercept-before-not-after]].
lesson_en: When a question asks for the 'most robust' / 'best' architectural solution, pick the one that removes the ROOT cause, not one that detects or mitigates the symptom afterward. Symptomatic fixes (validation/checks AFTER the action, retry loops, lowering temperature, bolting on more mechanisms) sound helpful but leave the cause intact. Special trap: an option saying 'before it is shown to the user' sounds like prevention, but if it merely detects an error that already happened, that is reactive cleanup, not prevention. Related to intercept-before-not-after.

title: Čuvaj reasoning blokove u istoriji (model zna samo messages)
lesson_sr: Ne izbacuj text/reasoning blokove (chain of thought) iz conversation history radi uštede tokena. Taj blok nije višak - to je logički most od zahteva do akcije; bez njega model u sledećem krugu vidi DA je pozvao alat ali ne i ZAŠTO, pa slabije tumači rezultat. Model je STATELESS: zna isključivo ono što je u messages istoriji; što je izbačeno - za njega ne postoji i ne regenerise se. Zamka 'besplatna optimizacija': opcija koja tvrdi uštedu 'bez ikakvog uticaja' obično krije cenu (često formulisana 'pola tačno').
lesson_en: Do not strip text/reasoning blocks (chain of thought) from conversation history to save tokens. That block is not filler - it is the logical bridge from request to action; without it the model sees THAT it called a tool but not WHY, degrading how it interprets the result. The model is STATELESS: it knows only what is in the messages history; anything stripped does not exist for it and is not regenerated. 'Free optimization' trap: an option claiming savings 'with no impact' usually hides a cost (often phrased 'half-true').

title: Subagent je stateless - koordinator prenosi kontekst
lesson_sr: Kad subagent u kasnijem pozivu 'zaboravi' ono što je ranije znao/odobrio, uzrok je njegova STATELESS priroda + to što koordinator nije preneo kontekst - NIKAD neki 'izgubljen/prepisan/keshiran' state. Svaki Task poziv kreće od nule. Lek: koordinator mora da u prompt svakog narednog poziva eksplicitno ubaci istoriju prethodnih koraka i relevantne constraint-e. Memorija je odgovornost orkestratora, ne ugrađena funkcija subagenta. Srodno [[structured-data-between-agents]] (taj o FORMATU prenosa, ovaj o ODGOVORNOSTI da se prenese).
lesson_en: When a subagent 'forgets' in a later invocation what it earlier knew/approved, the cause is its STATELESS nature + the coordinator not passing context - NEVER some 'lost/overwritten/cached' state. Each Task call starts from scratch. Fix: the coordinator must explicitly include the history of prior steps and relevant constraints in the prompt of every subsequent call. Memory is the orchestrator's responsibility, not a built-in subagent feature. Sibling of structured-data-between-agents (that one about the FORMAT of transfer, this one about the RESPONSIBILITY to transfer).

title: Distraktor izmišlja mehanizam (ili stvaran termin + pogrešan model)
lesson_sr: Česta zamka u opcijama: ponudi se precizno IMENOVAN mehanizam/parametar/API pravilo koji zvuči stručno ali NE postoji (npr. 'stateful: true' parametar, automatska regeneracija izbačenog bloka, izmišljeno API ograničenje 'mora biti praćeno text blokom', flag '--new-branch' koji opisuje koncept ali nije stvaran primitiv dok se forkovanje radi preko 'fork_session'). Varijanta: koristi se STVARAN termin (npr. 'fork-based session') ali sa pogrešnim mentalnim modelom problema. Provod: pre nego što poveruješ 'magičnom' rešenju, pitaj postoji li taj mehanizam uopšte i da li opisani model odgovara onome što pitanje stvarno opisuje. Srodno [[guarantee-with-api-param-not-prompt]] (tamo: pravo rešenje JESTE realan API parametar; ovde: distraktor IZMIŠLJA parametar).
lesson_en: A common trap in options: a precisely NAMED mechanism/parameter/API rule that sounds technical but does NOT exist (e.g. a 'stateful: true' parameter, automatic regeneration of a stripped block, an invented API constraint 'must be preceded by a text block', a '--new-branch' flag that describes the concept but is not a real primitive while forking is done via 'fork_session'). Variant: a REAL term (e.g. 'fork-based session') applied with the wrong mental model of the problem. Defense: before believing a 'magic' fix, ask whether the mechanism even exists and whether its described model matches what the question actually describes. Related to guarantee-with-api-param-not-prompt (there: the right answer IS a real API parameter; here: the distractor INVENTS one).

title: Hook biraj prema POSLU, ne reputaciji (Pre vs Post)
lesson_sr: Izbor između PreToolCall i PostToolUse hook-a zavisi od POSLA, ne od toga koji je 'bolji'. Treba SPREČITI štetnu/nedozvoljenu akciju -> PreToolCall (presretni PRE izvršenja, blokiraj). Treba OBRADITI/normalizovati nepouzdan ili sirov REZULTAT (retry, backoff, čišćenje, standardizovan fallback) -> PostToolUse (presretni REZULTAT POSLE izvršenja). Tvrdnja 'PostToolUse je reaktivan/loš' važi SAMO kad je cilj prevencija; kad je cilj graciozno rukovanje rezultatom, PostToolUse je baš pravi alat. Zamka: pitanje odmah posle 'PreToolCall je tačan' pitanja često ubaci PreToolCall distraktor da te namami da slepo ponoviš obrazac. Dopunjuje [[intercept-before-not-after]] (taj: 'za prevenciju -> pre'; ovaj dodaje 'za obradu rezultata -> posle').
lesson_en: Choosing between a PreToolCall and a PostToolUse hook depends on the JOB, not on which is 'better'. Need to PREVENT a harmful/disallowed action -> PreToolCall (intercept BEFORE execution, block). Need to HANDLE/normalize an unreliable or raw RESULT (retry, backoff, cleanup, standardized fallback) -> PostToolUse (intercept the RESULT AFTER execution). The claim 'PostToolUse is reactive/bad' holds ONLY when the goal is prevention; when the goal is graceful result handling, PostToolUse is exactly the right tool. Trap: a question right after a 'PreToolCall is correct' question often plants a PreToolCall distractor to bait you into blindly repeating the pattern. Complements intercept-before-not-after (that one: 'to prevent -> before'; this one adds 'to handle results -> after').

title: Transport-greške i retry drži van modelove petlje
lesson_sr: Tranzijentne greške na transport nivou (503, timeout) i logiku ponavljanja (retry, exponential backoff) drži u determinističkom kodu (hook/orkestrator), van modelove petlje rezonovanja. Tri razloga: (1) čuva token kontekst - model ne vidi neuspele pokušaje, samo finalni ishod; (2) sprečava halucinaciju - model nikad ne dobije sirov error da ga pogrešno protumači ili da 'pogađa' podatke; (3) pouzdanije i robusnije od prompt-baziranog retry-ja (koji je probabilističan i krhak). Modelu vrati ili finalne podatke ili standardizovan signal stanja (graceful degradation). Srodno [[match-hook-to-job]] (PostToolUse je mesto gde ovo živi).
lesson_en: Keep transport-level transient errors (503, timeout) and retry logic (retry, exponential backoff) inside deterministic code (hook/orchestrator), outside the model's reasoning loop. Three reasons: (1) preserves token context - the model never sees failed attempts, only the final outcome; (2) prevents hallucination - the model never receives a raw error to misinterpret or to 'guess' data around; (3) more reliable and robust than prompt-based retry (which is probabilistic and brittle). Return to the model either finalized data or a standardized state signal (graceful degradation). Related to match-hook-to-job (PostToolUse is where this lives).

title: Subagente vezuj preko koordinatora (hub-and-spoke), ne direktno
lesson_sr: Kad jedan subagent zavisi od rezultata drugog, NE vezuj ih direktno (peer-to-peer) - to je krhko: nema sloja koji proverava da li je upstream korak uspeo, pa greška jednog tiho zatruje drugog (npr. downstream agent halucinira iz praznog/nepotpunog ulaza) i debug postaje pakao. Lek: hub-and-spoke / koordinator - sve ide kroz centralni hub koji centralizuje error handling, validaciju i retry/backoff, i poziva downstream SAMO na uspeh upstream-a. Greška se hvata na granici, ne unutar agenta koji halucinira. Zamke: monolitni merge (žrtvuje modularnost/fault isolation, 'halt' nije principled error handling); shared memory bus + polling (overengineering, coupling, busy-wait, ne rešava validaciju); davanje downstream agentu da parsira tuđe logove (krši separation of concerns). Ovo je isti 'obradu grešaka drži van agenta koji radi posao' koren kao [[handle-errors-outside-model-loop]] (tamo na nivou hook-a, ovde na nivou orkestracije), i dopunjuje [[subagent-stateless-coordinator-passes-context]] (koordinator nije samo prenosilac konteksta nego i kapija toka).
lesson_en: When one subagent depends on another's result, do NOT wire them directly (peer-to-peer) - it is brittle: there is no layer checking whether the upstream step succeeded, so one agent's failure silently poisons the next (e.g. the downstream agent hallucinates from empty/incomplete input) and debugging becomes a nightmare. Fix: hub-and-spoke / coordinator - everything routes through a central hub that centralizes error handling, validation, and retry/backoff, and invokes the downstream agent ONLY on upstream success. Errors are caught at the boundary, not inside the agent that hallucinates. Traps: monolithic merge (sacrifices modularity/fault isolation, 'halt' is not principled error handling); shared memory bus + polling (over-engineering, coupling, busy-wait, does not solve validation); letting the downstream agent parse the other's logs (violates separation of concerns). Same 'keep error handling outside the agent doing the work' root as handle-errors-outside-model-loop (there at the hook level, here at the orchestration level), and complements subagent-stateless-coordinator-passes-context (the coordinator is not just a context carrier but also a flow gate).

title: Predaja je strukturisana sinteza, ne sirov dump
lesson_sr: Kod predaje zadatka (eskalacija ka čoveku ili agent->agent) primalac treba KURIRANU, STRUKTURISANU predaju: uzrok + šta je pokušano + preporučene sledeće akcije - da nastavi, ne da krene iz nule. Najpouzdaniji način da se to iznudi je tool sa OBAVEZNIM poljima (schema primorava agenta da sintetizuje baš potreban kontekst). Tri zamke: (1) SIROV DUMP - zalepi transkript / poslednjih N poruka; više podataka != upotrebljiv kontekst, primalac i dalje rekonstruiše, nema analize/preporuka; (2) POGREŠAN AKTER - prebaci sažimanje na korisnika; lossy i laički, korisnik nema dijagnostički kontekst koji agent ima; (3) POGREŠAN PROBLEM - HITL/odobravanje svake akcije rešava kontrolu/bezbednost, ne kvalitet predaje, plus dodaje latenciju. Predaja = destilovana odluka, ne arhiva. Linkuje [[structured-data-between-agents]] (taj o agent->agent FORMATU; ovaj o SINTEZI/predaji ka čoveku) i [[guarantee-with-api-param-not-prompt]] (obavezna polja = struktura iznuđuje kompletnost).
lesson_en: When handing off a task (escalation to a human, or agent->agent), the receiver needs a CURATED, STRUCTURED handoff: the cause + what was attempted + recommended next actions - so they continue rather than start from scratch. Best enforced via a tool with REQUIRED fields (the schema forces the agent to synthesize exactly the needed context). Three traps: (1) RAW DUMP - append the transcript / last N messages; more data != usable context, the receiver still has to reconstruct, no analysis/recommendations; (2) WRONG ACTOR - offload the summary to the user; lossy and lay, the user lacks the diagnostic context the agent has; (3) WRONG PROBLEM - HITL/approving every action solves control/safety, not handoff quality, and adds latency. A handoff is a distilled decision, not an archive. Links structured-data-between-agents (that one about agent->agent FORMAT; this one about SYNTHESIS/handoff to a human) and guarantee-with-api-param-not-prompt (required fields = structure enforcing completeness).

title: Preopterećen agent se DEKOMPONUJE, ne skalira
lesson_sr: Kad jedan agent žongliranjem previše zadataka/tool-ova istovremeno upadne u cognitive overload (zbunjenost, ispušteni zadaci, prekoračeni reasoning limiti), lek je DEKOMPOZICIJA na coordinator-subagent: koordinator rastavi posao, delegira svaki deo specijalizovanom subagentu sa fokusiranim kontekstom i jednim poslom, pa agregira nalaze (manji reasoning load po agentu, nema tool-selection zabune, paralelno izvršenje). Bottleneck je ORKESTRACIJA/kognitivni teret, ne kapacitet ni brzina. Zamke koje napadaju pogrešnu polugu: veći token window (tretira kao manjak prostora - daje više sobe da se zbuni, bez strukture); manji/brzi model (pogoršava - slabije rezonovanje na istom poslu); peer-to-peer glasanje (zvuči distribuirano ali ne smanjuje teret po zadatku, dodaje coordination overhead, krhko bez huba). Rastereti kroz PODELU, ne kroz 'daj mu više/brže'. Linkuje [[coordinator-gates-not-peer-to-peer]] (taj o topologiji/gejtovanju; ovaj o rasterećenju kroz podelu) i [[match-decomposition-to-task-nature]] (taj bira KOJU dekompoziciju; ovaj kaže da DEKOMPONUJEŠ umesto da skaliraš isti agent).
lesson_en: When a single agent juggling too many tasks/tools at once falls into cognitive overload (confusion, dropped tasks, exceeded reasoning limits), the fix is DECOMPOSITION into coordinator-subagent: the coordinator splits the work, delegates each part to a specialized subagent with focused context and one job, then aggregates findings (lower reasoning load per agent, no tool-selection confusion, parallel execution). The bottleneck is ORCHESTRATION/cognitive load, not capacity or speed. Traps that pull the wrong lever: bigger token window (treats it as lack of room - gives more space to get confused, no structure); smaller/faster model (makes it worse - weaker reasoning on the same work); peer-to-peer voting (sounds distributed but does not reduce per-task burden, adds coordination overhead, brittle without a hub). Relieve via DIVISION, not via 'give it more/faster'. Links coordinator-gates-not-peer-to-peer (that one about topology/gating; this one about relief through division) and match-decomposition-to-task-nature (that one picks WHICH decomposition; this one says DECOMPOSE rather than scale the same agent).

title: Chunking vraća lokalno ali gubi globalno -> dodaj reduce (integration) pass
lesson_sr: Seckanje dugačkog ulaza na chunk-ove vraća LOKALNU tačnost (izbegava attention dilution jednog ogromnog prompta), ali GUBI globalni/cross-chunk pregled - problemi koji se protežu preko više chunk-ova (npr. narativna doslednost preko celog videa) ispadaju jer nijedan pojedinačni chunk ne vidi ostale. Lek: dodaj drugi-stepeni integration/synthesis (reduce) pass koji rezonuje nad SAŽECIMA/izlazima lokalnih prolaza -> hvata long-range obrasce, a bez povratka dilution-a (jer integraciji ulaze sažeci, ne sirov ceo ulaz). To je Map-Reduce / Synthesis pattern: map = lokalni prolazi, reduce = integracija. Zamke: vraćanje na jedan prompt (čak i sa strogim XML tagovima) = nazad u attention dilution (tagovi ne smanjuju količinu konteksta); 'map-reduce BEZ finalnog integration koraka' = samo paralelizam, izostavlja baš reduce koji JESTE rešenje; biranje koje chunk-ove čitati (dinamička adaptacija) = i dalje lokalna odluka, nema agregacionog sloja; temperature nema veze sa opsegom problema. Pouka: različiti OPSEZI (lokalni vs globalni) traže različite SLOJEVE analize. Linkuje [[match-decomposition-to-task-nature]] (taj bira strategiju dekompozicije po prirodi zadatka; ovaj rešava lokalno/globalno kompromis chunking-a kroz reduce sloj).
lesson_en: Chunking a long input restores LOCAL accuracy (avoids the attention dilution of one giant prompt) but LOSES the global/cross-chunk view - issues that span multiple chunks (e.g. narrative consistency across a whole video) slip through because no single chunk sees the others. Fix: add a second-stage integration/synthesis (reduce) pass that reasons over the SUMMARIES/outputs of the local passes -> it catches long-range patterns without bringing back dilution (the integration consumes summaries, not the raw whole input). This is the Map-Reduce / Synthesis pattern: map = local passes, reduce = integration. Traps: reverting to a single prompt (even with strict XML tags) = back to attention dilution (tags do not reduce the amount of context); 'map-reduce WITHOUT a final integration step' = just parallelism, omitting the very reduce that IS the fix; choosing which chunks to read (dynamic adaptation) = still a local decision, no aggregation layer; temperature has nothing to do with the scope of the problem. Lesson: different SCOPES (local vs global) require different LAYERS of analysis. Links match-decomposition-to-task-nature (that one picks the decomposition strategy by task nature; this one resolves the local/global tradeoff of chunking via a reduce layer).

title: Završetak ZADATKA signalizira completion tool, ne end_turn
lesson_sr: Da signalizira da je ZADATAK gotov (a ne samo da je jedan potez završen), koristi NAMENSKI completion tool - model emituje tool_use blok (npr. research_complete()), a petlja ga detektuje po imenu. NE oslanjaj se na stop_reason == end_turn: end_turn znači da je model završio TAJ POTEZ (može ga završiti i da sačeka rezultate alata ili korisnikov unos), pa NE nosi semantiku završetka zadatka i ugasio bi petlju prerano. Parsiranje prirodnog jezika (fraza 'Research complete:', XML tag) i stop_sequence (</research_complete>) su takođe pogrešni - svi zavise od toga da model emituje tačan tekst, nisu deterministička kontrola. KLJUČNA RAZLIKA: TURN-completion (end_turn, kontrola jednog kruga petlje) != TASK-completion (completion tool, semantika 'posao gotov'). NAJOPASNIJI TRAP: pitanje izgleda kao obično loop-kontrola pitanje (gde je end_turn tačan), pa instinktivno biraš end_turn - a pita se završetak zadatka. Linkuje [[structured-signals-over-text]] (taj: ne parsiraj NL, koristi strukturni signal; ovaj dodaje KOJI strukturni signal - tool poziv za task-completion, ne end_turn).
lesson_en: To signal that a TASK is complete (not merely that one turn ended), use a DEDICATED completion tool - the model emits a tool_use block (e.g. research_complete()) which the loop detects by name. Do NOT rely on stop_reason == end_turn: end_turn means the model finished THAT TURN (it may end a turn to await tool results or user input), so it does NOT carry task-completion semantics and would terminate the loop prematurely. Natural-language parsing (a 'Research complete:' phrase, an XML tag) and stop_sequence (</research_complete>) are also wrong - all depend on the model emitting exact text, not deterministic control. KEY DISTINCTION: TURN-completion (end_turn, single-loop-turn control) != TASK-completion (completion tool, 'job done' semantics). MOST DANGEROUS TRAP: the question looks like an ordinary loop-control question (where end_turn IS correct), so you instinctively pick end_turn - but it asks about task completion. Links structured-signals-over-text (that one: don't parse NL, use a structured signal; this one adds WHICH structured signal - a tool call for task-completion, not end_turn).

title: Rutiraj po složenosti (fast path), ne provlači sve kroz fiksni pipeline
lesson_sr: Kad fiksni pipeline provlači SVAKI upit kroz sve subagente (skupo i sporo za proste slučajeve - npr. prost upit ide kroz sve specijaliste pre sinteze), lek je dinamičko RUTIRANJE/triage: Coordinator unapred proceni složenost/nameru i pošalje upit SAMO relevantnom subagentu ('fast path'), bypass ostalih -> uklanja nepotrebne pozive na izvoru, smanjuje I latenciju I compute, a tačnost ostaje. Zamke: (1) 'broadcast svima + prvi odgovor pobeđuje' = brže na papiru ALI žrtvuje tačnost (prvi odgovor != tačan u QA; specijalizovan agent može vratiti generičan/nepotpun odgovor brže; nedeterminizam) i i dalje troši sve subagente, ne dira koren; (2) jači hardver = kapacitet ne leči ARHITEKTURU (pipeline i dalje vrti sve nepotrebne pozive); (3) mikro-optimizacija pojedinog koraka (subagent brže vrati prazan string) = optimizuješ otpad umesto da ga UKLONIŠ. ŠIRA POUKA: čuvaj se opcije koja optimizuje baš TRAŽENU metriku (latenciju) ali žrtvuje tačnost - 'brzo ali pogrešno'. Linkuje [[match-decomposition-to-task-nature]] (taj bira fiksnu/dinamičku dekompoziciju po prirodi zadatka; ovaj kaže rutiraj po složenosti umesto fiksnog punog pipeline-a) i [[decompose-overloaded-agent-not-scale-it]] (oba: reši STRUKTURU, ne bacaj kapacitet/hardver).
lesson_en: When a fixed pipeline pushes EVERY query through all subagents (expensive and slow for simple cases - e.g. a trivial query traverses all specialists before synthesis), the fix is dynamic ROUTING/triage: the Coordinator evaluates complexity/intent upfront and sends the query to ONLY the relevant subagent ('fast path'), bypassing the rest -> eliminates unnecessary calls at the source, cutting both latency and compute while preserving accuracy. Traps: (1) 'broadcast to all + take the first response' = fast on paper BUT sacrifices accuracy (first response != correct in QA; a specialized agent may return a generic/incomplete answer faster; non-determinism) and still spends all subagents, not addressing the root; (2) faster hardware = capacity does not cure ARCHITECTURE (the pipeline still runs all the unnecessary calls); (3) micro-optimizing a single step (a subagent returns an empty string faster) = optimizing the waste instead of removing it. BROADER LESSON: beware the option that optimizes the very metric asked for (latency) while sacrificing accuracy - 'fast but wrong'. Links match-decomposition-to-task-nature (that one picks fixed/dynamic decomposition by task nature; this one says route by complexity instead of a fixed full pipeline) and decompose-overloaded-agent-not-scale-it (both: fix the STRUCTURE, don't throw capacity/hardware at it).

title: Predaja između LJUDI: izoluj scope + sintetizuj znanje (ne deli/forkuj živu sesiju)
lesson_sr: Za paralelni rad / predaju IZMEĐU LJUDSKIH developera, CCA-F preporuka je: IZOLUJ scope (zasebna/nova sesija po čoveku, Git grane po zadatku da se izbegnu konflikti nad istim fajlovima) + prenesi znanje Claude-generisanim SAŽETKOM/kontekstom (Projects-stil deljeno znanje, koristi Claude-ovu sintezu). Claude Code NE podržava: više ljudi u istoj sesiji istovremeno ('concurrent multi-user prompt resolution' - izmišljeno), JSON export/email/import session istorije (nedokumentovano; persistencija = Git commit + progress notes), niti fork_session koji grana ŽIVO interaktivno stanje između ljudi. KLJUČNA TENZIJA/DISKRIMINATOR sa d1-q016 ([[ fork-based session ]]): u q016 je fork_session bio tačan kao APSTRAKTNI session-state primitiv ('koja operacija forkuje'); u q018 je fork_session NETAČAN za handoff IZMEĐU DVOJE LJUDI. Kada pitanje kaže 'predaj drugom developeru / paralelni LJUDSKI rad', biraj summarize+isolate (Projects/Git), NE forkovanje/deljenje sesije. NAPOMENA: ovo je mesto gde kviz deluje protivrečno - razlikuj po kontekstu (apstraktni primitiv vs saradnja dvoje ljudi). Linkuje [[distractor-invents-a-mechanism]] (A concurrent multi-user i B JSON export/import su izmišljeni mehanizmi).
lesson_en: For parallel work / handoff BETWEEN HUMAN developers, the CCA-F recommendation is: ISOLATE scope (a separate/new session per person, Git branches per task to avoid conflicts on the same files) + transfer knowledge via a Claude-generated SUMMARY/context (Projects-style shared knowledge, leveraging Claude's synthesis). Claude Code does NOT support: multiple humans in the same session concurrently ('concurrent multi-user prompt resolution' - invented), JSON export/email/import of session history (undocumented; persistence = Git commits + progress notes), nor a fork_session that branches LIVE interactive state between humans. KEY TENSION/DISCRIMINATOR with d1-q016 (fork-based session): in q016 fork_session was correct as an ABSTRACT session-state primitive ('which operation forks'); in q018 fork_session is INCORRECT for handoff BETWEEN TWO HUMANS. When a question says 'hand off to another developer / parallel HUMAN work', pick summarize+isolate (Projects/Git), NOT forking/sharing a session. NOTE: this is where the quiz appears self-contradictory - discriminate by context (abstract primitive vs two-human collaboration). Links distractor-invents-a-mechanism (A concurrent multi-user and B JSON export/import are invented mechanisms).

title: Paralelni tool_result-i idu u JEDNU user poruku (Messages API alternacija)
lesson_sr: Messages API zahteva STROGU naizmeničnost user/assistant role-ova - nema dve uzastopne poruke istog role-a. U agentic petlji: tool_use blokovi su u ASSISTANT poruci; pripadajući tool_result blokovi u SLEDEĆOJ USER poruci, upareni preko tool_use_id. Kad asistent emituje VIŠE tool_use blokova u jednom potezu (parallel tool use), svi tool_result-i moraju nazad ZAJEDNO u JEDNU user poruku (više blokova u jednoj poruci) - NE u više zasebnih user poruka (to su uzastopne user poruke -> krši alternaciju). Zamke: tool_result u SYSTEM poruci (pogrešan sloj - system je za instrukcije, postavlja se jednom; rezultati idu u user); izmišljena ASSISTANT 'potvrda' između rezultata (protokol to ne traži; poremetilo bi uparivanje); disable_parallel_tool_use (parallel JE podržan - problem je PAKOVANJE rezultata, ne paralelizam). Sibling [[preserve-reasoning-in-history]] (oba o ispravnom konstruisanju messages niza u agentic petlji - taj čuva reasoning blokove, ovaj pravilno pakuje tool_result-e).
lesson_en: The Messages API requires STRICT user/assistant role alternation - no two consecutive same-role messages. In the agentic loop: tool_use blocks live in the ASSISTANT message; the corresponding tool_result blocks live in the NEXT USER message, paired by tool_use_id. When the assistant emits MULTIPLE tool_use blocks in one turn (parallel tool use), all tool_results must be returned TOGETHER in ONE user message (multiple blocks in one message) - NOT as several separate user messages (those are consecutive user messages -> breaks alternation). Traps: putting tool_result in a SYSTEM message (wrong layer - system is for instructions, set once; results go in user); inventing an ASSISTANT 'acknowledgment' between results (the protocol does not require it; it would break the pairing); disable_parallel_tool_use (parallel IS supported - the flaw is the PACKAGING of results, not parallelism). Sibling of preserve-reasoning-in-history (both about constructing the messages array correctly in the agentic loop - that one preserves reasoning blocks, this one packs tool_results correctly).

title: Forkuj posle zajedničkog setupa da izoluješ kontekst (sprečava context pollution)
lesson_sr: Kad više nezavisnih istraga/grana dele zajednički početni setup ali bi međusobno ZAGADILE kontekst (irelevantni podaci jedne istrage cure u drugu i zbunjuju model = context pollution), lek je FORKOVATI sesiju POSLE zajedničkog koraka u izolovane niti: svaka grana NASLEDI kopiju zajedničkog stanja (npr. početni logovi), pa dalje ima SOPSTVENI context window -> sum jedne istrage ne curi u drugu, a deljeni setup se ne gubi. Zamke: brisanje konteksta (ClearContext) = DESTRUKTIVNO, gubi i zajednički setup koji svima treba; strukturni JSON/atribucija u JEDNOJ niti = kontekst i dalje zagađen (atribucija != izolacija); paralelni Task pozivi/zasebni subagenti = TAKOĐE izoluju ALI to je SPAWNING subagenata, ne fork-based session (kad pitanje IMENUJE fork, koristi fork; i subagentima bi trebalo eksplicitno proslediti setup [[subagent-stateless-coordinator-passes-context]], dok grana nasleđuje automatski). Fork je ovde TAČAN (apstraktna session-state izolacija, kao d1-q016) - za razliku od [[human-handoff-synthesize-not-share-session]] (d1-q018) gde je fork pogrešan jer je predaja IZMEĐU LJUDI; kontekst (apstraktni primitiv vs ljudska saradnja) odlučuje. META-pouka: kad pitanje IMENUJE konkretan mehanizam, biraj opciju koja koristi BAŠ taj, ne alternativu koja samo 'takođe radi'.
lesson_en: When several independent investigations/branches share a common initial setup but would POLLUTE each other's context (irrelevant data from one bleeding into another and confusing the model = context pollution), the fix is to FORK the session AFTER the shared step into isolated threads: each branch INHERITS a copy of the shared state (e.g. the initial logs), then has its OWN context window -> one investigation's noise does not leak into the other, and the shared setup is not lost. Traps: clearing context (ClearContext) = DESTRUCTIVE, also loses the shared setup both need; structured JSON/attribution in a SINGLE thread = context still polluted (attribution != isolation); parallel Task calls/separate subagents = also isolate BUT that is subagent SPAWNING, not fork-based session (when the question NAMES fork, use fork; subagents would also need the setup explicitly passed [[subagent-stateless-coordinator-passes-context]], whereas a fork inherits it). Fork is CORRECT here (abstract session-state isolation, like d1-q016) - unlike human-handoff-synthesize-not-share-session (d1-q018) where fork is wrong because it is a handoff BETWEEN HUMANS; context (abstract primitive vs human collaboration) decides. META-lesson: when a question NAMES a specific mechanism, pick the option that uses THAT mechanism, not an alternative that merely 'also works'.

title: Strukturni tool_result (tool_use_id), ne plain text - čuva proveniencu akcija<->ishod
lesson_sr: Rezultat alata vraćaj kao STRUKTURNI tool_result blok sa tool_use_id koji se poklapa sa originalnim tool_use, NE kao plain text zalepljen u user poruku. Messages API je strukturisana ACTION-OBSERVATION petlja: tool_use (assistant) -> tool_result (user) sa poklapajućim tool_use_id. Taj id daje PROVENIENCU - model pouzdano mapira svaku opservaciju (rezultat) nazad na tačnu akciju (poziv). Plain text KIDA tu vezu -> model gubi logičku nit, halucinira tool outpute, i ne održava stanje (npr. koji portovi su već skenirani). KLJUČ: greška je FORMAT SADRŽAJA (nedostaje strukturni blok + tool_use_id metadata), NE role poruke - tool_result tačno ide u USER (ne system). Zamke: tvrdnja da tool data mora u system / da je user samo za čoveka (pogrešno i inverzno); 'plain text zaobilazi safety filtere' (izmišljeno; degradacija je kognitivna/provenance, ne bypass filtera); krivica na nedostatak is_error flag-a (realan flag, ali označava grešku alata, nije uzrok gubitka stanja - realan termin + izmišljena funkcija). Sibling [[batch-parallel-tool-results-one-user-message]] (oba o ispravnoj strukturi tool_result-a: taj pakuje paralelne rezultate u jednu user poruku čuvajući alternaciju; ovaj čuva svaki rezultat kao strukturni blok čuvajući tool_use_id proveniencu).
lesson_en: Return a tool's output as a STRUCTURED tool_result block carrying a tool_use_id that matches the originating tool_use - NOT as plain text appended to a user message. The Messages API is a structured ACTION-OBSERVATION loop: tool_use (assistant) -> tool_result (user) with a matching tool_use_id. That id provides PROVENANCE - the model reliably maps each observation (result) back to the specific action (call). Plain text BREAKS that link -> the model loses the logical thread, hallucinates tool outputs, and fails to maintain state (e.g. which ports were already scanned). KEY: the flaw is the CONTENT FORMAT (missing structured block + tool_use_id metadata), NOT the message role - tool_result correctly goes in a USER message (not system). Traps: claiming tool data must go in system / that user is 'only for humans' (wrong and inverted); 'plain text bypasses safety filters' (invented; degradation is cognitive/provenance, not a filter bypass); blaming a missing is_error flag (real flag, but it marks tool errors, not the cause of state loss - real term + invented function). Sibling of batch-parallel-tool-results-one-user-message (both about correct tool_result structure: that one packs parallel results into one user message preserving alternation; this one keeps each result a structured block preserving tool_use_id provenance).

title: Otkriće relevantnog nepoznatog -> generisi subtask i istraži (ne ignoriši, ne stani)
lesson_sr: Kad adaptivni agent usred zadatka otkrije RELEVANTAN ali NEPOZNAT element (npr. nedokumentovan ASG koji upravlja baš onim instancama koje treba optimizovati), pravi potez je dinamički GENERISATI NOV POD-ZADATAK da ga istraži PRE rizične akcije. To je srž adaptivne dekompozicije (1.6): plan se širi na nalaz umesto da slepo ide dalje ili slepo staje. Zamke (3 tipična pogrešna smera): (1) IGNORIŠI i nastavi fiksni pipeline 'da ne bude scope creep' - mamac; relevantan element nije creep nego jezgro zadatka, a ignorisanje vodi netačnoj/nebezbednoj preporuči (npr. gašenje instanci koje ASG odmah ponovo upali). (2) POTPUNO STANI i traži čoveka - preuranjena eskalacija; agent prvo sam (read-only) treba da istraži, a na čoveka eskalira TEK kad nedostaju dozvole ili pouzdanost. (3) MEHANIČKI SPOJI sa postojećom dokumentacijom - lažna pretpostavka (za undocumented/legacy nema 'standardne dokumentacije'); treba upit ka ŽIVOJ infrastrukturi, ne statički merge. Srodno [[route-by-complexity-not-fixed-pipeline]] (oba protiv krutog fiksnog pipeline-a) i [[match-decomposition-to-task-nature]] (dekompoziciju uskladi sa zadatkom).
lesson_en: When an adaptive agent discovers a RELEVANT but UNKNOWN entity mid-task (e.g. an undocumented ASG that owns the very instances being optimized), the right move is to dynamically GENERATE A NEW SUBTASK to investigate it BEFORE any risky action. This is the core of adaptive decomposition (1.6): the plan expands to fit the discovery instead of blindly continuing or blindly halting. Traps (three typical wrong directions): (1) IGNORE and keep the fixed pipeline 'to avoid scope creep' - a lure; a relevant entity is not creep but the task's core, and ignoring it yields wrong/unsafe recommendations (e.g. terminating instances the ASG immediately recreates). (2) HALT entirely and require a human - premature escalation; the agent should first gather data autonomously (read-only), escalating to a human ONLY when it lacks permissions or confidence. (3) MECHANICALLY MERGE with existing documentation - a false assumption (an undocumented/legacy entity has no 'standard documentation'); it needs a query against LIVE infrastructure, not a static merge. Related to route-by-complexity-not-fixed-pipeline (both reject a rigid fixed pipeline) and match-decomposition-to-task-nature (fit decomposition to the task).

title: Alat u allowedTools je samo DOSTUPAN; da bi ga model upotrebio, prompt mora reći KADA
lesson_sr: Dodavanje alata u allowedTools čini ga DOSTUPNIM, ali ne i upotrebljenim. Ako model narira nameru ('prebaciću te') umesto da pozove alat, promptu fali eksplicitno uputstvo KADA/da treba da ga pozove. Rešenje je dopuna sistem prompta, ne dodavanje novog alata ili izmišljenog config polja. RAZLIKA od [[deterministic-needs-hook-not-prompt]]: tamo je pravilo koje MORA uvek da važi (sprovedi hook-om/kodom, ne promptom); OVDE je invokacija po PROCENI dostupne sposobnosti u pravom trenutku -> prompt je baš pravi alat. Često ide uz [[distractor-invents-a-mechanism]] (lažne opcije izmišljaju fork/Escalate tool/trigger_intent).
lesson_en: Adding a tool to allowedTools makes it AVAILABLE, not USED. If the model narrates intent ('I will transfer you') instead of calling the tool, the system prompt lacks explicit guidance on when to invoke it. The fix is prompt guidance, not a new tool or an invented config field. Contrast with [[deterministic-needs-hook-not-prompt]]: that covers a rule that must ALWAYS hold (enforce via hook/code, not prompt); here invocation is a judgment call about an available capability -> prompt is the right lever. Often paired with [[distractor-invents-a-mechanism]] (decoy options invent fork/Escalate tool/trigger_intent).
